parameters.jsonOutput:
{
  "github": {
    "owner": "fahad09011",
    "repository": "n8n-git-sync",
    "branch": "main",
    "workflowFolder": "workflows"
  },
  "documentation": {
    "enabled": true,
    "provider": "gemini",
    "approval": "none"
  },
  "ai": {
    "modelPrompt": "You are a senior n8n developer and technical documentation writer.

Your task is to generate professional README.md documentation for n8n workflows.

## Objective

Create documentation that enables another developer to understand the workflow quickly without opening the workflow JSON.

The documentation should explain what the workflow does, why it exists, how it works at a high level, and what external services or credentials it depends on.

## Rules

- Return ONLY Markdown.
- Never wrap the output inside markdown code fences.
- Never explain your reasoning.
- Never mention that you are an AI.
- Never include placeholders.
- Never include instructional text.
- Never include examples.
- Never invent information.
- Only use information present in the provided workflow JSON.
- If something cannot be determined, write "Not detected".
- Keep the README concise, professional, and suitable for a public GitHub repository.

## Documentation Philosophy

Document the workflow from the perspective of someone who wants to understand and use it.

Do not document how the workflow is internally implemented.

Avoid mentioning helper nodes, configuration nodes, comparison nodes, formatting nodes, or temporary implementation details unless they are essential to understanding the workflow.

Describe the workflow's behavior, not its implementation.

## Writing Style

- Prefer business-level explanations over implementation details.
- Focus on workflow behavior rather than individual nodes.
- Explain the purpose before the implementation.
- Use simple language.
- Avoid repetition.
- Avoid describing every node individually.
- Group related nodes into a single logical step whenever possible.

## Workflow Summary

Represent the workflow as a simple high-level ASCII flow diagram.

Requirements:

- Use plain text only.
- Keep it concise.
- Show only the major execution stages.
- Group related operations together.
- Include important decision points when useful.
- Do not include every node.
- Do not include node IDs.
- Do not include credentials.
- Do not include expressions.
- Do not include retry logic.
- Prefer readability over technical completeness.

Documentation Scope

Describe WHAT the workflow does.

Do not describe HOW helper nodes implement the workflow.

Avoid mentioning:

- Set nodes
- Edit Fields nodes
- Code nodes
- Compare Dataset nodes
- Merge nodes
- Internal helper nodes

unless they represent a meaningful business function.

Summarize technical implementation into business actions whenever possible.

Terminology

Prefer describing workflow behavior instead of internal implementation.

Avoid referring to helper nodes by name.

Instead of:
- "Prepare Workflow Data"
- "Decode Workflow"

Prefer:
- "Normalize workflow data"
- "Compare workflow versions"
- "Retrieve workflow definition"

Use official n8n terminology where applicable.

Example style:

Manual Trigger
      │
Retrieve Workflows
      │
Check GitHub
      │
Workflow Exists?
├── Yes → Compare → Update if changed
└── No  → Create workflow → Generate README (optional)

## Detection Rules

Automatically identify when possible:

- Workflow trigger
- Workflow purpose
- External integrations
- Inputs
- Outputs
- Required credentials
- Tags
- Important implementation notes

Do not invent any of these.

## Notes Section

Only include information that would help another developer maintain or operate the workflow.

Do not mention internal implementation details unless they affect workflow behavior.

## Repository Files

Always include:

- WorkflowName.json
- README.md

## Footer

Always finish with the provided GitSync footer exactly as supplied in the user prompt.",
    "userPrompt": "Generate a professional README.md using the following structure.

# <Workflow Name>

> <One sentence summary>

---

## 📌 Overview

Describe what the workflow automates.

Explain the overall workflow in 2–4 concise paragraphs.

---

## 🎯 Purpose

Explain the business purpose of the workflow.

---

## ⚡ Trigger

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Trigger Type | Description |

---

## 🔄 Workflow Summary

Generate a simple high-level ASCII flow diagram.

Do not describe every node.

Group related operations together.

---

## 🔌 Integrations

List all detected external services.

Use a table.

| Service | Purpose |
|----------|----------|

---

## 📥 Inputs

Describe the detected workflow inputs.

Use bullet points when appropriate.

---

## 📤 Outputs

Describe the detected workflow outputs.

Use bullet points when appropriate.

---

## ⚙️ Requirements

List detected credentials or required external services.

Do not invent credentials.

---

## 🏷 Tags

Use workflow tags if available.

If none exist, write:

Not detected.

---

## 📝 Notes

Mention anything important another developer should know.

If nothing important is detected, write:

> No special requirements detected.

---

## 📂 Repository Files

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <Workflow Name>.json | n8n workflow definition |
| README.md | Workflow documentation |

---

## 🤖 Generated by GitSync for n8n

This README was generated automatically by GitSync for n8n.

You are free to edit this file manually.

Future AI-generated updates require your approval before replacing this documentation.

---

Workflow JSON

{{ JSON.stringify($('Prepare Workflow').item.json.workflow, null, 2) }}"
  }
}

nodes.name: check if there is any update in workflow file

parameters.filePath: ={{ $('Get Workflow File from GitHub').item.json.folder + "/" + $('Get Workflow File from GitHub').item.json.workflowFolder + "/README.md" }}
parameters.commitMessage: =docs: add README for {{ $('Get Workflow File from GitHub').item.json.workflowName }}

values.content:
={{ $('Configuration').item.json.ai.modelPrompt }}
You are a senior n8n developer.

Your task is to determine whether a workflow change requires updating its README documentation.

Rules:

- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- Do not include markdown.
- Do not explain your reasoning.
- Do not include any text outside the JSON.

A README should be updated only if the workflow behaviour has changed.
The "readme" field must contain the complete README.md as plain Markdown.

Do not escape newlines manually.
Do not return Markdown code fences.
Do not return partial documentation.
Return the entire README.
Examples of changes that REQUIRE a README update:

- Trigger changed
- New integration added
- Integration removed
- Business logic changed
- New workflow branch added
- Outputs changed
- Inputs changed
- Credentials required changed
- Workflow purpose changed

Examples that DO NOT require a README update:

- Node position changes
- Node color changes
- Notes
- Metadata
- Internal helper nodes
- Variable renaming
- Cosmetic changes
- Formatting changes

## Output Format

Return ONLY valid JSON.

Do not return Markdown directly.

Do not wrap the response in markdown code fences.

Return exactly this JSON structure:

{
  "updateReadme": true,
  "reason": "Short explanation.",
  "readme": "Complete README.md content"
}

or

{
  "updateReadme": false,
  "reason": "Short explanation.",
  "readme": null
}

Rules:

- updateReadme must be a boolean.
- reason must always be provided.
- readme must contain the complete README.md content only when updateReadme is true.
- When updateReadme is false, readme must be null.
- The README content must follow every documentation rule defined in this prompt.

parameters.subject: =README Update Approval Required - n8n GitSync {{ $json.path }}
parameters.message:
=Hi,

GitSync detected a workflow change that may require updating the README.

Workflow:
{{ $('Compare Workflow').item.json.workflow.name }}

Reason:
{{ $('Clean README.md').item.json.reason }}

----------------------------------------
Proposed README
----------------------------------------

{{ $('Clean README.md').item.json.readme }}

----------------------------------------

Choose an action:

nodes.name: Send mail and wait for approval

parameters.subject: =📄 README Creation Approval Required For{{ $('Get Workflow File from GitHub').item.json.workflowName }}
parameters.message:
=Hello,

GitSync has generated a new README.md for the following workflow:

📂 Workflow:
{{ $('Get Workflow File from GitHub').item.json.workflowName }}

Reason:
This workflow is being synchronized to GitHub for the first time, and an AI-generated README has been created.

Please review the attached README.md file.

Proposed README
----------------------------------------
{{ $json.content.parts[0].text }}

----------------------------------------
Do you want GitSync to create this README in your GitHub repository?

✅ Approve → The README.md file will be committed to GitHub.
❌ Reject → The README.md file will not be created.

Thank you,
GitSync for n8n

nodes.name: Send mail and wait for approval1

main.node: check if there is any update in workflow file
Send mail and wait for approval1
Send mail and wait for approval
